beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning wake-up after a low-energy Saturday (35/100); Mason starts slow, easing into the day with caffeine and a calm routine.

beats.activity: Drive to Green Lake Park for morning walk and stargazing notes review
beats.reasoning: Perfect sunny Sunday weather invites outdoor time; Green Lake is close to Maple Leaf and offers a natural transition into the day. Mason loves stargazing and may review notes from recent night-sky sessions.

beats.activity: Walk around Green Lake, take film photos, enjoy the calm morning
beats.reasoning: Sunday calm, low energy from yesterday, and perfect weather make this a recharge moment. Mason brings his film camera everywhere and values reflective time over crowds.
Sunday is a lighter work day; Mason heads to the studio for a late-morning session or to do admin and design prep work.

beats.activity: Studio work: sketch designs, prep for a client session
beats.reasoning: Light Sunday schedule; Mason uses the morning for creative work rather than full back-to-back sessions, matching the calm rhythm of the day.

beats.activity: Lunch walk through Fremont, grab a bite at a local spot
beats.reasoning: Sunny day, light energy; Mason enjoys standing outside with food, same pattern as recent days. Fremont is walkable and familiar.
Leisurely return; Mason may shoot more film photos in afternoon light, maintaining the reflective Sunday pace.
One afternoon session fits a light Sunday; not the back-to-back grind of weekdays.

beats.activity: Drive to Volunteer Park for sunset and reflection
beats.reasoning: Perfect sunny weather, good energy from session; Mason recreates yesterday's solo dinner moment but moves it earlier to catch the golden hour. Values this reflective ritual.

beats.activity: Sit at Volunteer Park, take photos, watch light change, maybe grab a casual bite nearby
beats.reasoning: Sunset time, perfect conditions; Mason's favorite solo decompression ritual. Pairs well with his film camera practice and need for quiet after a morning of socializing.
Calm drive home; Mason avoids evening crowds and settles in before dark.

diary: Woke up easy this morning—72 degrees and clear skies, the kind of Sunday that reminds you why you stay in Seattle. Grabbed coffee at home, then drove out to Green Lake for my walk. I've been tracking stargazing notes for a while now, and this morning felt right to review them while the park was still quiet. Walked the loop with my film camera, just letting the light do its thing. No rush. Got to the studio by late morning, spent a couple hours sketching out designs and prepping for my afternoon client. Molly Moon's for lunch—hit the spot. The session went smooth, nice clean lines on a piece that took real focus. But honestly, the best part was driving over to Volunteer Park after work. Sat there as the light shifted from gold to pink to that deep blue. Took some photos, but mostly just watched. Felt grounded, you know?
thought_process: Today felt like the kind of day I needed without planning it that way. I started with intention—the stargazing notes, the early park walk—but it turned into something less structured and more meditative. The weather helped; there's something about 72 and sun that just settles you. Work was solid, no complications with the client, which meant I could actually be present instead of problem-solving. The decision to go to Volunteer Park instead of heading straight home felt important. I think I've been craving that kind of stillness lately, that moment where you're not doing anything but existing in good light. It's the opposite of how the week usually moves—this felt like permission to slow down.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
